prevalence.value: common; ~50-70% of children have at least one episode by age 3
prevalence.trend: stable; pneumococcal vaccination reduces some bacterial burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute onset of symptoms plus middle-ear effusion and signs of inflammation (bulging TM); otoscopy is key; differentiate from otitis media with effusion

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: very high pediatric episode burden annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with appropriate analgesia and selective antibiotics; stewardship needed

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in APS; otoscopy quality varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Analgesia first; antibiotics (amoxicillin) for selected cases by age/severity; follow-up for recurrent cases and hearing concerns
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic overuse and limited access to ENT for recurrent cases in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of amoxicillin and strong pediatric guidelines
